Patient-facing workflow stage: Before visit / digital or paper intake
Patient action: Choose language/accessibility mode; enter top concerns, symptom timeline, change from normal, daily impact, main fear/theory, family history, medications, prior tests and out-of-system visits.
System action: Detect emergency language and redirect; prepare concise intake summary; mark missing risk-context fields.
Clinician handoff: Nurse/doctor sees patient words plus structured timeline, not only chief complaint.

Patient action: Confirm symptoms are same/worse/new; indicate deterioration while waiting; provide caregiver/family concern if desired.
System action: Show repeat-visit and high-risk-history banner; start waiting-time and deterioration checks.
Clinician handoff: Registration/nurse gets plain-language red flags and escalation button.

Patient action: Verify top concern and what has changed; repeat if symptoms worsen.
System action: Pair acuity score with diagnostic uncertainty; prompt repeat vitals/reassessment timers when needed.
Clinician handoff: Nurse sees red flags, recent visits, vitals trends and patient/caregiver concern.

Patient action: Review/confirm timeline, patient quote and unresolved concerns.
System action: Show doctor-facing summary, missing data checklist, prior results/referrals and dangerous-to-miss prompts.
Clinician handoff: Doctor sees concise source-linked timeline and can edit/override.

Patient action: See disposition for each concern, pending tests, what remains unexplained, safety-net instructions and who owns follow-up.
System action: Create follow-up tasks and pending-test contract; collect patient correction if summary misses something.
Clinician handoff: Doctor/nurse closes encounter only with owner/deadline for pending items.

Patient action: Answer “symptoms still present/worse?” check-ins; view result/referral status; request escalation if deterioration is not being addressed.
System action: Escalate unresolved symptoms, overdue abnormal results, uncompleted referrals and repeat visits.
Clinician handoff: Worklist routes overdue actions to owner/backup; patient status is visible.

Patient action: Maintain longitudinal symptoms, baseline changes, records/photos and out-of-system care.
System action: Trend labs/symptoms and keep unresolved diagnostic threads visible.
Clinician handoff: Specialists and primary care share one diagnostic episode view.
